questions.title: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) had _____ Daughters.
questions.explanation:
Holy Prophet had Four Daughters from Hazrat Khadija.

  1. Hazrat Zainab (married to Abual Bin Aas Bin Rabbi)
  2. Hazrat Ruqayah (married to Hazrat Usman bin Affan)
  3. Hazrat Umme Kalsoom (married with Hazrat Usman bin Affan after the death of Hazrat Ruqayah)
  4. Hazrat Fatima (married to Hazrat Ali)


Additional Information:

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons 
  1. Hazrat Qasim 
  2. Hazrat Abdullah
  3. Hazrat Ibrahim 

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

questions.title: When does a pilgrim perform Umrah in Hajj Tamattu'?
questions.explanation:
Hajj Tamattu enters Ihram on the eighth day of Dhul-Hijjah

The pilgrim who performs Hajj-ul-Tamattu is called a Mutamatti. Hajj-ul-Tamattuj

It means to perform Umrah during the season of Hajj.

In Hajj-ul-Tamattuj a pilgrim assumes Ihram for only Umrah.

After reaching Makkah, he performs Tawaf and Saa’ee for Umrah.

questions.title: What is the Islamic law regrading the protection of human life?
questions.explanation:
• Qisas refers to retributive justice in Islamic law, ensuring fair punishment for crimes like murder.
  • Sanctity of human life and right to self-defense are fundamental principles in Islam, emphasizing protection and justice.

questions.title: What is the first obligation of Islam?
questions.explanation:
• Shahada is the first pillar of Islam.
  • It affirming belief in the oneness of Allah and the prophethood of Muhammad (PBUH).
  • It is the fundamental declaration that makes one a Muslim: "There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is His Messenger."

questions.title: Which surah contains the longest verse in the Holy Quran, detailing the laws and principles of financial transactions?
questions.explanation:
• The longest verse in the Quran is Ayat al-Mudayanah (Verse 282) of Surah Al-Baqarah.
  • It details financial transactions, including the importance of written agreements and witnesses in debt contracts.
  • It emphasizes honesty, clarity, and record-keeping in financial dealings to prevent disputes.

questions.title: In which battle did the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) strictly enforce the rule that non-combatants, such as women and children, should not be harmed, setting an example of warfare in Islam?
questions.explanation:
• In the Battle of Badr (624 CE), Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) emphasized ethical warfare, strictly prohibiting harm to non-combatants, including women, children, and the elderly.
  • This battle set a precedent in Islamic military ethics, promoting justice and humane treatment even during conflict.

questions.title: In which Surah of the Holy Quran, rights of the women have been explained?
questions.explanation:
• Surah Al-Talaq (The Divorce) discusses women's rights in matters of divorce, maintenance, and waiting period (Iddah).
  • It provides guidance on fair treatment and financial responsibilities towards women after divorce.

questions.title: The Battle of Safeen (Saffin) was fought in 37 A.H. between Hazrat Ali (RA) and _______?
questions.explanation:
The Battle of Siffin took place in 657 CE (37 AH).

It was fought between Caliph Ali ibn Abi Talib and Mu'awiya ibn Abi Sufyan, the governor of Syria.

questions.title: The Mosque of Prophet ﷺ was damaged due to fire in the reign of ______?
questions.explanation:
Abu Isḥaq Muḥammad ibn Harun al-Rashid, better known by his regnal name al-Muʿtaṣim biʾllāh, was the eighth Abbasid caliph, ruling from 833 until his death in 842.

A younger son of Caliph Harun al-Rashid, he rose to prominence through his formation of a private army composed predominantly of Turkic slave-soldiers.

questions.title: How many times Holy Prophet (PBUH) went to Allah with the request to reduce the number of Salats until these remained only five?
questions.explanation:
Initially, Allah commanded 50 daily prayers, but after the Prophet's (PBUH) repeated requests, the number was gradually reduced to 5.

This event occurred during the Isra and Mi'raj (Night Journey and Ascension), a significant miracle in Islamic history.

questions.title: The payments after the death of a pilgrim are given to:
questions.explanation: If a pilgrim passes away, any due payments, refunds, or compensation are handed over to their close relative as per Hajj policies.

questions.title: Complaint, if not even relevant, according to the rules, should be:
questions.explanation:
• Even if a complaint is irrelevant, it must still be recorded according to rules.
  • Recording complaints ensures transparency and accountability, allowing for future reference and potential review.
  • It prevents unfair dismissals and maintains a formal complaint tracking system.

questions.title: On 10th Zilhaj, the ritual of Rami (stoning the devil) is performed at which Jamarat?
questions.explanation:
On 10th Dhul Hijjah, pilgrims perform Rami al-Jamarat (stoning the devil) at Jamarah Uqbah (the largest pillar).


It is performed on four consecutive days of the Hajj, the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th days of Dhul-Hijjah (the 12th Islamic Month).

On the first day, pilgrims cast seven stones at Jamarat al-Aqaba.

questions.title: Which company has the contract for traveling during Hajj?
questions.explanation:
• Tawafh Company is responsible for managing transportation for Hajj pilgrims in Saudi Arabia.
  • It ensures smooth bus services, logistics, and movement between Mina, Arafat, Muzdalifah, and Makkah.

questions.title: After 9 PM, does anyone enter into the Hujjaj room?
questions.explanation:
• There are strict regulations regarding privacy and security in the Hajj accommodations.
  • After 9 PM, entry is restricted to ensure rest and order among pilgrims.

questions.title: Hujjaj offer Eid-ul-Adha Prayer on which day?
questions.explanation:
• Pilgrims (Hujjaj) do not offer Eid-ul-Adha prayer because they are engaged in Hajj rituals on the 10th of Dhul Hijjah.
  • Instead of the prayer, pilgrims perform Rami al-Jamarat (stoning of the devil), sacrifice an animal (Qurbani), and shave their heads.

questions.title: Hujjaj can change their assigned rooms by?
questions.explanation:
• Once a room is assigned during Hajj, it cannot be changed due to strict logistics and management.
  • This ensures fair accommodation distribution and avoids mismanagement.

questions.title: Pilgrims can go through the Airports after?
questions.explanation:
• Pilgrims must pass through customs clearance at the airport before departure.
  • Other processes like immigration, security checks, and document verification are also necessary but customs clearance is the final step before proceeding.

questions.title: Where is OPAP (Office for Pilgrim Affairs of Pakistan) situated?
questions.explanation:
• OPAP (Office of Pilgrim Affairs of Pakistan) is located in Azizia, a district in Makkah, Saudi Arabia.
  • This office handles Hajj-related services, complaints, accommodations, and support for Pakistani pilgrims.

questions.title: In case of medical illness, approach:
questions.explanation:
• During Hajj, Pakistan Mission Hospitals provide medical assistance to Pakistani pilgrims.
  • Other options like Hajj Operators or Pilgrims’ Affairs Offices may assist in arrangements but do not provide medical treatment.

questions.title: Which day is called Yawm-e-Tarwiyah?
questions.explanation:
Yawm-e-Tarwiyah is the first day of Hajj

It is the 8th day of Dhul-Hijjahr

When pilgrims travel to Mina and prepare for the journey.

The pilgrim put on his Ihraam after the Salaat of Zohr & Asr and head out of Makkah to Mina.

He spends the rest of the day and night praying in Mina, preparing to set out to Arafaat.

questions.title: How much affidavit does a woman needs to submit to go to Hajj alone
questions.explanation:
• According to the latest Hajj policies in many countries, including Pakistan, a woman aged 45 years or above can perform Hajj without a Mahram if she submits an affidavit stating that she will travel with an organized group.
  • The affidavit is usually on a Rs. 50 stamp paper confirming her consent and travel arrangements.

questions.title: Which app is used to determine the correct route during hajj?

  • "Pak Hajj Guide" may provide information about Hajj but does not serve as a navigation tool.

questions.title: On which days of Dhul-Hijjah is the ritual of Jamarat performed?
questions.explanation:
The Stoning of the Devil ritual is performed at the Jamarat,

which is located in Mina, near Mecca.

It is performed on four consecutive days of the Hajj, the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th days of Dhul-Hijjah (the 12th Islamic Month).

On the first day, pilgrims cast seven stones at Jamarat al-Aqaba.

questions.title: What color jackets are worn by Pakistani Muavineen (assistants) during Hajj?
questions.explanation: The Muavineen (assistants) from Pakistan who assist pilgrims during Hajj wear green jackets or coats to distinguish themselves and make it easier for pilgrims to identify and seek help from them.

questions.title: What color bracelet is provided by the Saudi government to pilgrims during Hajj?

The act governing Hajj regulations in Pakistan in 2023 is called the Hajj and Umrah (Regulation) Act, 2023.

The act was approved by Parliament and will be implemented after the rules and regulations are formulated.

questions.title: What kind of action by the pilgrims, if abandoned, requires paying a penalty (دم)?
questions.explanation:
Suppose a pilgrim abandons a Wajib (mandatory) action during Hajj or Umrah.

In that case, it requires a penalty (دم) to be paid, which usually involves sacrificing an animal or other compensatory actions.

questions.title: What is the status of the Sai between Safa and Marwah?
questions.explanation:
Sa'i between Safa and Marwah is an essential rite (wajib)


Running b/w Safa & Marwa seven times is called Sayee. 

Muslims travel back and forth between them 

It is an integral part of the Hajj and Umrah in Islam.

After performing Say’ee Hujjaj go to Mina.

**

It is done to symbolize Hajar's (Wive of Prophet Ibraheem, Peace be upon them both) search for water for her son Prophet Ismael (Peace be upon Him).

questions.title: What is the status of the Tawaf al-Wida (Farewell Tawaf)?
questions.explanation:
It is a farewell Tawaf and is performed as a final ritual of Hajj when a pilgrim leaves Makkah.

It is wajib.

To perform Tawaf Al-Wada, Muslims are directed to circumambulate the Holy Kaaba seven times and pray two Rakats (only if one wants to, as it is not mandatory).

questions.title: The tax collected from prisoner is called _____?

1. Jizya: A tax historically levied on non-Muslims living under an Islamic state, in exchange for protection and exemption from military service. It has no connection to prisoners.

  2. Fidya: Compensation for failing to fulfill certain obligations like fasting. This is unrelated to imprisonment.

  3. Ashar: A tithe (1/10th tax) on agricultural produce, unrelated to prisoners.

In Islam, prisoners are generally entitled to humane treatment and are not burdened with taxes for their captivity.

If there are costs incurred by a prisoner (e.g., restitution for crimes), this would be part of the judicial process and not termed as a "tax."

questions.explanation:

questions.title: Which ritual during Hajj involves standing on the plain of Arafat?
questions.explanation:
The ritual during Hajj that involves standing on the plain of Arafat is called "Wuquf" or "Waqoof" at Arafat.

It is considered one of the most significant and essential rituals of Hajj pilgrimage,

where pilgrims gather and stand in supplication and worship from noon until sunset on the 9th day of Dhu al-Hijjah.

questions.explanation:

This act is typically performed after the completion of the major rituals of Hajj,

such as the standing at Arafat and the stoning of the pillars in Mina.

questions.title: What is the significance of the Black Stone (Hajar al Aswad) located in Kaaba?
questions.answer: It marks the starting point of tawaif
questions.explanation:
The Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad) in the Kaaba marks both the starting and ending point of each circumambulation (Tawaf)

during Hajj and Umrah pilgrimages, symbolizing the spiritual journey of Muslims and their connection to the Kaaba.

Its significance extends beyond mere ritual, embodying unity, historical legacy, and spiritual blessings in Islam.

شاہ ولی اللہ رحمہ اللہ کے نزدیک قرآن مجید کی پانچ منسوخ آیات درج ذیل ہیں:

سورۃ البقرہ (2:180)

سورۃ البقرہ (2:240)

سورۃ النساء (4:15)

سورۃ الانفال (8:65)

سورۃ المجادلہ (58:12)

questions.title: اخوان المسلمین کا وطن کون سا تھا؟
questions.explanation: اپنے آغاز میں اس تنظیم کا مقصد اسلامی اخلاقیات کا پرچار تھا لیکن جلد ہی یہ سیاست میں شامل ہو گئی

questions.title: Hazrat Ibrahim was the son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat ____?
questions.explanation:
Ibrahim was the third son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Only 1 son (child) was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA) 

Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Born in Dhu al-Hijjah 8 AH 
He has died at the age of 2 on Shawwal 29, 10 AH

He was buried in Jannat al-Baqi, Medina


Additional Information:

Hazrat Maria was the 11th wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ

She was 36 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 60 years ago

All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

questions.title: Hazrat Umar R.A martyred in which month of Islamic Calendar?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Umar Farooq was martyred on 24 A.H 1st Muharram

****

Full name: Umar ibn Al-Khattāb

Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second Rashidun caliph, ruling from August 634 until his assassination in 644 (13 to 24 Hijri).

the wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) Hazrat Hafsah (رضہ) was the daughter of Umar Farooq (R.A)

*****

questions.explanation:

questions.title: What is the name of the father of Hazrat Umar (R.A)?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second caliph of Islam.

He was ruled for 10 years from 634 CE to 644 CE.

He succeeded Hazrat Abu Bakr.

He was instrumental in expanding the Islamic state.

questions.title: For how years Hazrat Abdul Muttalib (R.A) took care of Prophet (SAW)?
questions.explanation:
• Abd al-Muttalib Shaybah ibn Hashim, born in 497 A.D and died in 578 A.D
    • He was the grandfather of Prophet Muhammad (SAW).
    • He took care of the Prophet (SAW) for 2 years after the death of his mother.

questions.title: Usher is levied on ____?
questions.explanation:
• Usher is an Islamic tax levied on agricultural produce, usually amounting to one-tenth (10%) of the harvest.
  • It is considered a form of Zakat.
  • It is meant to support the needy and promote social welfare.

questions.title: Which was the 2nd major Ghazwa of Islam?
questions.explanation:
• The Battle of Uhud, the second battle of Islam.
    • It was fought in 3 Hijri (625 AD).
    • Abu Sufyan was the commander of the infidels.
    • Ohad is the name of a mountain.
    • In this battle, 1,000 Muslims faced 3,000 infidels.

questions.title: How many months prior to the birth of Hazrat Muhammad (SAW), Hazrat Abdullah lbn-e-Abdul Matalib died?
questions.explanation: حضرت محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی ولادت سے چھ ماہ قبل حضرت عبداللہ بن عبدالمطلب کی وفات ہوئی۔

questions.title: Which topic was discussed in Surah-e-Qadar?
questions.explanation:
• Surah Al-Qadar discusses the revelation of the Quran on Laylat-ul-Qadr (The Night of Decree).
  • It highlights the significance of this night, stating it is better than a thousand months (Quran, Surah Al-Qadr: 3).

questions.title: Who among the following requested Allah to free him till the day of judgment?
questions.explanation:
• Iblees (Satan) requested Allah for respite until the Day of Judgment after refusing to bow to Prophet Adam (AS).
  • Allah granted him the time to mislead humans as a test for mankind (Quran, Surah Al-A'raf: 14-15).

questions.title: What is the meaning of Al-Wahid?
questions.explanation:
Wahid, meaning "peerless", "unique".

Al-Wahid is one of the 99 names of Allah.

questions.title: Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Declared _______ key to Paradise.
questions.explanation:
Jabir ibn Abdullah reported:

The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said,

“The key to Paradise is prayer, and the key to prayer is ablution.”

questions.title: What is the name of Surah of Holy Quran which mentions the rights of the parents?
questions.explanation:
In Surah Luqman, Verse 14, it says: "We have enjoined upon man to be kind to his parents."

Similarly, in Surah Al-An'am, Verse 151, it says: "You have to be kind to your parents."

questions.title: In which battle was the trench dug?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Salman Al-Farsi suggests digging the trench at the time of the Battle of Khandaq

The Battle of Khandaq (Ahzab) was fought on 5 A. H (627).

Ghazwa e Ahzab and Battle of the Trench is the second name of Ghazwa-e-Khandak 

Muslims 3000

Kufar 10000

10000 Munafiqeen were in Ghazwa-e-Khandaq

It was the 30-day-long fight of Yathrib by Arabian and Jewish tribes
